# deep-search

A guided training loop that turns a plain-English failure-mode description plus labeled runs into a signal, vs `signals` where the trained detector then lives. Dashboard: Deep Search.

**When to use:** Train a new detector from examples: describe a failure mode, label the runs that match it, and mint a classifier from those labels.

## Commands

| Command | Invocation | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `create-classifier-signal` | `bentolabs deep-search create-classifier-signal <session_id> --data '<json>'` | train a signal from the session's match labels; needs >=1 match |
| `create-session` | `bentolabs deep-search create-session --data '<json>'` | open a training run from a query + scope; mints the session_id |
| `get-session` | `bentolabs deep-search get-session <session_id>` | re-read status, iteration, resulting signal_id |
| `label-candidate` | `bentolabs deep-search label-candidate <session_id> --data '<json>'` | mark one trajectory match/no_match/skip; re-labeling overwrites |
| `list-candidates` | `bentolabs deep-search list-candidates <session_id> [--limit --cursor]` | trajectories ranked by similarity to the query; labeled ones drop off |

Confirm exact arguments with `bentolabs deep-search <command> --help`.
